title: External-test-runner execution provenance (A2 — framework-generated)
description: run-provenance.json — written by the ExternalTestRunnerExecutor from the REAL managed subprocess (argv, pid, real wall-clock, exit code, tool version, spec sha256) and stamped with the DRIVER's invocation_nonce (handed in via env). It is a real-execution attestation, NOT a deterministic artifact: it deliberately carries real timestamps so a byte-identical hand-authored replay is impossible. The driver's pre-spawn provenance gate validates: schema-valid; invocation_nonce == manifest.provenance == paired e2e_start/e2e_end audit events; wall_clock_start/end ⊆ [e2e_start_ts, e2e_end_ts]; exit_code consistent with the normalized criterion results; and the Audit-Spine chain verifies — else gate_hard_fail before any Acceptance verdict. Missing/inconsistent ⇒ fail closed.

argv.description: The exact managed runner command spawned (structured, shell=False).

pid.description: OS pid of the managed runner process.

exit_code.description: Real subprocess exit code (non-zero when tests failed); reconciled against the normalized criterion results. MUST be a concrete integer — a null exit code is incomplete real-subprocess provenance and is rejected fail-closed.

wall_clock_start.description: ISO-8601 UTC real start of the managed run.

wall_clock_end.description: ISO-8601 UTC real end of the managed run.

spec_sha256.description: sha256 of the adopter spec that ran (empty when no single spec_path).

invocation_nonce.description: The DRIVER-generated per-run nonce (framework-owned). An adopter-supplied nonce can never match the driver's marker/events, so a hand-authored provenance cannot satisfy the gate.
